pending.label: Corren - Letter 2 (what happened to Mira)
pending.variant:
Mira.

The soul-sand was cold.

I do not remember the heat after.

You said something.

I think you said my name.

I think you said Run.
I did not turn around.

- Corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 3 variant A (Bram's laugh)
pending.variant:
Bram.

You laughed like falling.

Sudden. Loud. Always too loud.

You said you laughed so the dark would know you were there.

It always did.

- Corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 3 variant B (Bram's shield)
pending.variant:
Bram.

Your shield was too big for you.

You walked sideways through doors.

Esa called it the table.

You said the table never let anyone down.

- Corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 4 variant A (the first one is yours)
pending.variant:
Bram.

The first skull was yours.

You picked it up.

You shouted something.

I could not hear over the fire.

The wheels on this train make three notes.

The first one is yours Bram.

- Corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 4 variant B (it weighed nothing)
pending.variant:
Bram.

i wrote this five times.

each time i get to the skull and the pen stops.

you held it up. you grinned.

you said it weighed nothing.

i think it weighed a lot.

The wheels on this train make three notes.

The first one is yours Bram.

- Corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 5 variant A (her hands)
pending.variant:
Esa.

Your hands were cold from brewing.

The rest of you was warm.

Mira said you were the only warm one of us.

She was right.

- Corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 5 variant B (her songs)
pending.variant:
Esa.

You sang while you bandaged.

Old songs. None of us knew them.

You said you made them up as you went.

We pretended to believe you.

- Corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 6 variant A (the push)
pending.variant:
Esa.

Your hand was on my back.

Cold from brewing.

You pushed once.

I stumbled to safety.

You did not push again.

You were already gone, I think.

Before the push.

- Corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 6 variant B (the one who lived)
pending.variant:
Esa.

There are people on this train who have lived longer than me.

none of them look right.

i do not want to know what i look like.

you would have told me.

you would have said sit down Corren and let me see.

- corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 7 variant A (The Train)
pending.variant:
I do not know how I got out.

The portal was broken. I remember that.

I do not remember walking through.

I came up in a field. Tall grass. The train was already there.

It was moving.

It had always been moving. I know that now.

I ran beside it. I jumped for the nearest door.

I do not know how I caught it.

I thought I had made it.

I have been making it for weeks now.

The train does not stop.

There is no off.

- Corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 7 variant B (related facts)
pending.variant:
the heat behind me. the cold ahead.

i did not know which way was up.

i remember falling onto grass.

the grass was not on fire.

that seemed important.

then a building.

i did not know the building.

i know all the buildings in this region.

i mapped this region.

a train was at the building.

the doors were open.

i was alive and the doors were open.

these felt like related facts.

- corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 8 variant A (the screech and the blasts)
pending.variant:
I have heard it for weeks now.

A screech. Three voices, almost layered.

Sometimes one comes alone. Sometimes all three.

And the blasts.

I feel them in the floor before I hear them.

They never break anything I can see.

I do not know where they land.

I walk forward.

The screech comes again. Two carriages back.

Always from there.

Never further.

I am sure.

- Corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 8 variant B (what the wheels do not carry)
pending.variant:
there is a sound.

i have heard it for weeks.

it is not the wheels.

the wheels carry music. three notes.

this one carries something else.

it screams.

three voices. almost in time. never quite.

and there are blasts.

i feel them in the floor. they do not break anything.

i have asked the others on the train.

none of them hear it.

only i hear it.

it is for me.

it has time.

- corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 9 variant A (the fourth voice)
pending.variant:
Oh.. oh no.

Mira.

You would have noticed it before I did.

The screech is in front of me now.

There is not supposed to be a front.

Bram would have stood in front.

He would have laughed at it.

He always laughed at things that were too big to fight.

Esa would have brewed something.

Fire resistance. Swiftness. The right thing.

She always brewed the right thing.

I walked forward for weeks.

I was walking toward it.

The train has no front.

Every step forward was a step closer.

I can see it now.

Three heads. Three voices.

The fourth voice will be mine.

I think it has been mine for weeks.

- Corren

pending.label: Corren - Letter 9 variant B (three of you)
pending.variant:
its here.

mira.

it was not behind me.

it was in front.

it was always in front.

you would have seen this. you saw everything first.

bram would have stood in front of me.

he would have laughed.

he laughed at things too big to fight.

it would have worked. for a moment.

esa would have put her hand on my back.

cold.

she would have pushed.

i would have gone.

i walked toward it.

i thought i was walking away.

the train has no front. you would have laughed at me for forgetting that.

i can see it.

three heads. three of you.

bram. esa. you.

i am the fourth.

i have been the fourth all along.

- corren

pending.label: Edda - Letter 1 variant A (a seat that is not mine)
pending.variant:
My name is Edda Marsh.

I am writing this from a seat that is not mine.

My seat was three carriages behind. I left it quickly. Not in fear. Closer to the way you leave a room when the kettle has been on too long.

I will explain when I have walked further. For now: I have moved. I have begun moving.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 1 variant B (the unfolded shawl)
pending.variant:
Edda Marsh.

This seat is warm. Not from me.

My seat is back the way I came. Three carriages, perhaps four. I did not count.

My shawl is still on it. Folded. Folding was the last thing I did not do.

More when I have walked further.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 2 variant A (three heads at the old window)
pending.variant:
I am in a sleeping car. The bed has been made. Not by me.

Last night something was at my old window.

Three heads. Bone the colour of wet ash. The middle larger than the others.

They moved one at a time. The middle would still and the two beside it would turn. All three looked in.

I did not look away. There seemed no reason to. After a while they were gone.

I waited an hour. Then I got up. Then I walked.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 2 variant B (I have looked at worse)
pending.variant:
A sleeping car. The bed is made. Not by me.

Three heads at the old window last night.

Bone-grey. Middle one larger. The two beside it turned when it did not.

They all looked in.

I have looked at worse things in my life. I looked back.

After a while they were gone. I waited. I walked.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 3 variant A (the library car experiment)
pending.variant:
I walked forward four carriages. I am writing from a reading nook in what may be a library car. Some of the books are real. Some are spine-only and glued to the wall.

It is here too. At my window. Not the one I left. This new one.

The same three heads. The middle one tilted slightly off centre, as if listening.

I walked to the other side of the car. That window was dark. I came back. It was at mine again.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 3 variant B (bright with bone)
pending.variant:
Four carriages forward. A library car. Some of the books are real. Some are spines glued to the wall.

It is here too. At this window. Not the one I left.

Same three heads. Middle one tilted, as if listening.

I crossed the car. The other window was dark. I came back. Mine was bright with bone again.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 4 variant A (Hollin, soul sand, what came up)
pending.variant:
I am in a goods car. The crates are nailed shut. The light is the only thing not boxed.

I should tell you about Hollin.

He was my husband. He dug soul sand. He sold most of it. He kept a little for himself.

He dug too deep, in the end. Something came up out of the place he was digging.

Three heads. A middle one larger than the others. A tilt to the middle one. As if listening.

Hollin did not come up.

I have been waiting a long time to see that face again. Now I have.

It is at every window I sit by.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 4 variant B (Hollin stayed)
pending.variant:
A goods car. Crates nailed shut. The light is the only thing not boxed.

Hollin.

He was my husband. He dug soul sand. Sold most. Kept a little.

He dug too deep. Something came up.

Three heads. Middle one larger. Tilted. As if listening.

Hollin stayed.

I waited a long time to see that face again.

Now I have.

Edda

pending.label: Edda - Letter 5 variant A (the windows show what we carry)
pending.variant:
I am in an observation car. Long windows. Many of them. A few other passengers.

I sat here for hours. I watched the windows. Not out of them. At them.

The train moves at a steady pace. The view does not. A tree stays beside us for too long. A river runs the wrong way and then runs ours.

The next carriage had its own view. A different valley. The carriage after that had a field I knew when I was twelve.

There is a man two seats down who is watching a kitchen. I think it is his mother's kitchen. He has been crying very quietly for an hour.

The windows do not show outside. They show what the watcher carries.

I have been carrying Hollin. I have been carrying what came up out of his digging.

It is not following me. It has always been mine.

I am steady. I want you to know I am steady.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 5 variant B (not following, mine)
pending.variant:
Observation car. Long windows. Several. A few other passengers.

I watched the windows. Not out of them. At them. For hours.

The train moves at a steady pace. The view does not. A tree stays too long. A river runs the wrong way and then runs ours.

Next carriage: a different valley. The one after: a field I knew when I was twelve.

Two seats down a man is watching a kitchen. I think it is his mother's. He has been crying very quietly.

The windows show what we carry.

I have been carrying Hollin. And what came up out of his digging.

It has always been mine. Not following. Mine.

I am steady.

Edda Marsh

pending.label: Edda - Letter 6 variant A (lifted the latch, for whoever finds the seat next)
pending.variant:
I have stopped walking.

I am in a small carriage near the back. Or near the front. The light is the same in both directions.

There is a window. There is a latch on the window. I have been looking at the latch for a long while.

The three heads are here. They are not at the glass now. They are a little way out from it, in the dark. Waiting.

I have decided I am not going to walk again. I have decided I am going to lift the latch.

I have lifted the latch.

The window is open. The night is very cold. The cedar smells stronger when the air moves through it.

I am going to leave this here. I am going to leave the window open too. For whoever finds the seat next.

pending.label: Edda - Letter 6 variant B (leaving the window open too)
pending.variant:
I have stopped.

A small carriage. Near the back. Or the front. The light is the same in both directions.

A window. A latch. I have been looking at it a long while.

The three heads are here. Not at the glass now. A little way out. Waiting.

I am not going to walk again. I am going to lift the latch.

I have lifted it.

The window is open. The night is cold. The cedar smells stronger when the air moves.

Leaving this here.

Leaving the window open too.

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 41 variant A (Edda Maer interview)
pending.variant:
Sighting Forty-One.

Witness: Edda Maer. Carriage 114. Long aboard.

Reports: figure in the doorway. Eyes white. Train at full motion. Five seconds. Gone.

Cross-questioned three times. Story did not move. Detail held.

The witness will not retract. Forty entries did. This may be the forty-first.

- H. Pent

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 41 variant B (the laugh)
pending.variant:
Sighting Forty-One.

A man across from me asked what I was writing.

I said: field notes.

He said: about what.

I said: the white-eyed man.

He laughed.

It was the laugh of a person who has never seen him. I have catalogued the laugh.

- H. Pent

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 43 variant A (stubborn witness Pell Cordoba)
pending.variant:
Sighting Forty-Three.

Witness: Pell Cordoba. Insists.

I offered her: lamp afterimage. Carriage flicker. Eye fatigue from the tunnels.

She said: I have lived underground. I know lamps.

I offered her: a man wearing eye protection. Some mining cults do.

She said: he had no shadow. The lanterns made shadows. He did not.

I closed the entry without an explanation.

This is unusual for me.

- H. Pent

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 43 variant B (four witnesses, same sentence)
pending.variant:
Sighting Forty-Three.

Today I have interviewed four. Independent. No cross-contamination.

Carriage 87: white eyes through the door glass.

Carriage 154: white eyes through the door glass.

Carriage 226: white eyes through the door glass.

Carriage 401: white eyes through the door glass.

I have written the same sentence four times.

Four different witnesses wrote it before me.

- H. Pent

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 48 variant A (Joren Vask, hypnagogic theory)
pending.variant:
Sighting Forty-Eight.

Witness: Joren Vask. Carriage 519.

Could not finish the interview. Twice put down the pen and stared at the floor.

Three witnesses today have done this. Same posture. Same delay.

Hypothesis: prolonged tunnel exposure produces a recurring hypnagogic figure. The white eyes are the afterimage of carriage lamps on the cone cells.

The trauma is real. The figure is not.

This is the most coherent theory I have constructed in years.

- H. Pent

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 48 variant B (folie a deux at scale)
pending.variant:
Sighting Forty-Eight.

Working hypothesis.

Sustained tunnel-flicker. Disrupted circadian rhythm. Shared narrative exposure between strangers.

These conditions produce a folie a deux at scale. The white-eyed man is the train's local hallucination. Every long-haul population invents one.

The trauma is real. The figure is shared because the conditions are shared.

This is the entry I have been waiting to write for eleven years.

- H. Pent

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 61 variant A (clocking the witness)
pending.variant:
Sighting Sixty-One.

I entered Carriage 47. A woman was eating.

I knew before she looked up.

The way she held the spoon. The way her eyes did not move toward the door glass when the train flickered.

I sat down. I said: when did you see him.

She said: yesterday.

The interview took two minutes.

I am very good at this.

- H. Pent

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 61 variant B (catalogue of signs)
pending.variant:
Sighting Sixty-One.

The signs I now recognise:

The flinch at carriage flicker. The half-second of held breath at a door opening. The avoidance of door glass.

The way they hold their cup with two hands.

I enter a carriage and read the room.

Three witnesses today. None disagreed with my opening question.

Eleven years. This is the year it has all paid off.

- H. Pent

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 78 variant A (Sora Penn, letting the lie stand)
pending.variant:
Sighting Seventy-Eight.

Witness: Sora Penn. Carriage 488.

Claims she has seen nothing.

She has the spoon-hold. The door-glass avoidance. The half-second of held breath.

She has every sign in my notebook.

I thanked her for her time. I did not contradict her.

She wanted to be left thinking she was fine.

I left her thinking that.

- H. Pent

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 78 variant B (the quiet, no verb fits)
pending.variant:
Sighting Seventy-Eight.

Some of them are trying to hide it now.

They mention the name without meaning to. They look at door glass and then look away too fast.

One man said the word "shadow" three times in five minutes. Unprompted.

I do not press. I write the entry afterward.

I used to think I was studying them.

I am no longer sure what the verb should be.

- H. Pent

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 92 variant A (unrecordable, Witness: myself)
pending.variant:
Sighting Ninety-Two.

Witness: myself.

Carriage 612. Door glass. Late.

Something stood. Or did not.

The carriage flickered. The figure flickered with it. Then neither.

This is the kind of entry I have closed in retraction forty times.

I cannot record eye colour. I cannot record duration. I cannot record absence of shadow.

I can record that I stopped walking.

I record the witness response. This is one.

- H. Pent

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 92 variant B (marginal collapse, brackets)
pending.variant:
Sighting Ninety-Two.

Witness: H. Pent.

[The interviewer is also the witness. This invalidates the entry.]

Two carriages back. Through the door glass. A figure. Eyes white. Five seconds.

[The entry is recorded anyway.]

I cannot trust my own observation. I cannot dismiss it either.

I have spent the night in the corridor.

- H.P.

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 140 variant A (methodical archive search)
pending.variant:
Sighting One Hundred & Forty.

Returned to cabin. Opened archive crate.

I am looking for my own sighting in someone else's entry. A precedent.

Cross-ref: Sighting 17. Sighting 22. Sighting 41.

Sighting 17 — witness could not record duration. Could not record eye colour. The match is exact.

It should be a relief.

It is not. The date line is in different ink. I do not remember changing pens.

Probably nothing.

- H. Pent

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 140 variant B (Sighting 17 quoted excerpt)
pending.variant:
Sighting One Hundred & Forty.

What I saw last week — if I saw anything — should be in here somewhere. A precedent. Some other witness with the same gaps.

I found Sighting 17.

"Witness reports figure in mine shaft. Eyes white. Six seconds. Subject cannot account for the time after."

I do not remember interviewing this person.

The phrasing is mine. The handwriting is mine.

The dates do not line up with my field trips that year.

Probably nothing.

- H.P.

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 211 variant A (the dates are wrong)
pending.variant:
Sighting Two Hundred & Eleven.

The dates are wrong.

Sighting 22 is dated three months before I was hired.

Sighting 31 describes the train. I did not know the train existed.

Sighting 41 describes Edda Maer. I met Edda four weeks ago.

I have not slept. The number above this entry is a guess.

The notebook is not what I thought it was.

- H.P.

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 211 variant B (notebook contains my future)
pending.variant:
Sighting Two Hundred & Eleven.

I am holding a notebook that contains my future.

Sighting 41, dated nine years ago, reads:

"Witness: Edda Maer. Carriage 114. Long aboard."

I wrote that sentence last week.

[The page has aged.]
[The binding is original.]
[The ink is mine.]

I have stopped knowing how to date these entries.

- H.P.

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 240 variant A (interview reversed, dining carriage)
pending.variant:
Sighting Two Hundred & Forty.

A woman sat across from me in the dining carriage.

She asked: have you seen him.

She looked at me the way I have looked at four hundred people.

I said: I do not know what you mean.

It was Sora Penn's line. I wrote it down four weeks ago. I am hearing it in my own voice.

I did not finish the meal. I returned to my cabin. I have not opened the door since.

The notebook is open.

- H.P.

pending.label: Halen - Sighting 240 variant B (catalogued, paranoia)
pending.variant:
Sighting Two Hundred & Forty.

I have been catalogued.

They look at me the way I taught myself to look at them.

The way they hold their cups. The small slow turns toward door glass.

In the corridor today, a man stepped back from me. He had the recognition.

He did not ask. He knew.

I will not write what he knew.

I will not write what I saw.

I am writing about the notes.

- H.

pending.label: Halen - Sighting — variant A (composed acceptance, finale)
pending.variant:
Sighting —

I have stopped numbering.

I went back to the earliest entries.

Sighting 1 is dated thirty-seven years ago.

I am thirty-seven.

Sighting 1 is in my handwriting. The same loop on the y. The same comma habit.

I do not remember being six.

I do not remember being eight, ten, twelve. I remember adult things. I remember nothing before the field.

On the inside of the back cover, in a hand I have never seen, are five words:

"I gave you the notebook."

I have been reading and writing this notebook all of my life. I did not write this line.

I do not know who did.

- H. Pent

pending.label: Halen - Sighting — variant B (fragmented confession, finale)
pending.variant:
Sighting —

[I have stopped numbering.]

The earliest entry. Sighting 1.

The date. Thirty-seven years ago.

I am thirty-seven.

[The handwriting is mine.]

[I do not remember being six.]
[I do not remember being twelve.]
[I do not remember being twenty.]

The first thing I remember is the field. I was already a folklorist. I had already been writing in this notebook.

Inside the back cover, written in a hand I do not know:

"I gave you the notebook."

I have been answering it my whole life.

-

pending.label: Soll Letter 1 variant A (loadout, S. Marra)
pending.variant:
Day One. Loadout.

Iron sword, x2 (one back-up per the notebook).
Bow, 64 arrows.
Enchanted leather, full set, Protection I.
Ender pearls, x16.
Bed, x1.
Raw potatoes, x32.

The notebook calls for gold apples. The town had none. I will source them en route.

The rat is real. I have logged him.

Day One.

- S. Marra

pending.label: Soll Letter 1 variant B (loadout, snuck out)
pending.variant:
Day One. Loadout.

Iron sword, x2.
Bow, 64 arrows.
Enchanted leather, Prot I.
Ender pearls, x16.
Bed.
Potatoes, x32.

The notebook says gold apples are non-negotiable. The town had none. I left before anyone noticed I was gone.

A rat in a vest gave me this paper. He looked sad. I do not know what to log him as.

I am ready.

- Soll Marra

pending.label: Soll Letter 2 variant A (notebook, bringing it)
pending.variant:
The notebook is Halric's. He started it twenty-six years ago, the year before he met me.

Specs for the End. Pillar heights. Crystal cages. Cage break order. Bed-blast yields. Pearl trajectories.

He never went.

He died in spring. Lung-thing. The notebook calls it "cessation, non-combat."

The dragon never met Halric.

I am bringing it to her.

- S. Marra

pending.label: Soll Letter 2 variant B (notebook, bringing him)
pending.variant:
The notebook is Halric's. He started writing it the year before I was born. Twenty-six years of specs and no dragon at the end.

Pillar heights. Crystal cages. Cage break order. Bed-blast yields. Pearl trajectories. The whole fight, on paper.

He died in spring. The notebook calls it "cessation, non-combat." I think it was his lungs. He coughed a lot. He never said the word.

The dragon never met Halric.

I am bringing him to her.

- Soll

pending.label: Soll Letter 3 variant A (drill one, what ready feels like)
pending.variant:
Drill One. Carriage 14.

Target: zombie, no armour.
Range: 9 blocks.
Cover: a bench.
Bow: full draw, two seconds.

Halric's perch-cycle adapts. The carriage lamp stands in for the crystal. Drop the crystal first. Pivot. Loose.

Result: one shot. Notebook target time eleven seconds. Mine, four.

The notebook does not say what ready feels like.

- S. Marra

pending.label: Soll Letter 3 variant B (drill one, the lamp swung)
pending.variant:
Drill One. Carriage fourteen.

I picked a zombie because the notebook starts with crystals and I do not have a crystal. The lamp on the wall was the right shape.

Bench cover. Nine blocks. Full draw. Pivot. Loose.

The zombie went down in one. Halric's target time was eleven seconds. I took four.

I was ready, then. Then I sat on the bench and the carriage was quiet and the lamp swung a little.

I do not know what I expected.

- Soll

pending.label: Soll Letter 4 variant A (sighting, fourth apprentice)
pending.variant:
Carriage 28. Encounter.

Passenger: female, mid-fifties, grey wool coat. Apparently real.

She asked my name. I gave Halric's title. Apprentice of Halric.

She said, "Halric. So you're his fourth."

I did not know there had been three.

I noted her seat number. I moved on.

Sighting closed.

- S. Marra

pending.label: Soll Letter 4 variant B (witness, fletcher with cough)
pending.variant:
Carriage twenty-eight. A woman in a grey coat asked my name.

I gave Halric's title. Apprentice of Halric.

She said, "Halric? The fletcher with the cough?"

I did not know he was a fletcher. I have one of his bows in my pack.

I should have sat down. I should have asked her what else she knew. I did not. I walked to the next carriage and I am sitting here writing.

She is still in seat 14. I think.

- Soll

pending.label: Soll Letter 5 variant A (eye of ender, train not in a direction)
pending.variant:
Attempt 47. Eye of ender.

Throw vector: forward, twenty-five degrees off horizontal. Per notebook page nine.

The eye held position. Six seconds. It rotated counter-clockwise, then resolved.

Bearing: north-by-north-east.

I changed carriage. I threw again.

Bearing: north-by-north-east.

The carriage faces south.

The eye is reading a direction. The train is not in a direction.

I have stopped throwing.

- S. Marra

pending.label: Soll Letter 5 variant B (eye of ender, will not fly)
pending.variant:
The eye of ender will not fly.

I threw it forty-seven times. Forward. Up. Sideways. Out the door.

The eye hovers. It spins. It does not pick a direction. Halric wrote that an eye always picks. The eye, page nine: "the only honest thing in a stronghold approach."

It is being honest. There is no stronghold.

I sat with the eye in my hand. It was warm. It is not warm now.

I do not know which way I am going.

- Soll

pending.label: Soll Letter 6 variant A (closed notebook, Soll Marra)
pending.variant:
I have closed the notebook.

The cover is brown leather. There are names inside the front board. I did not look at them.

I am writing on the back page. Halric did not write here.

I am nineteen. I have never seen a dragon. I do not want to kill her.

I am going to keep walking until I find a window with the right kind of light. Then I am going to sit.

Soll Marra

pending.label: Soll Letter 6 variant B (closed notebook, Soll)
pending.variant:
I closed the notebook.

The back page is empty. I sat with it open for a long time.

Halric is not in the back page. Halric is in the front pages. I have read them too many times.

I am nineteen. I have never seen a dragon. I do not want to kill her.

I do not know where this train is going. I think that is alright.

I am going to find a window.

Soll
